full_response:
Step 1 Write the two events in the primed (Cartesian) coordinates  
• Event O (the “origin”): (t′,x′,y′,z′) = (0,0,0,0)  
• Event P on Γ : (t′,x′,y′,z′) = (x₀ sinh (at), x₀ cosh (at), y₀, z₀)

Here t is any real parameter along the curve Γ, while x₀ , y₀ , z₀ are fixed constants.

Step 2 Form the coordinate differences  
Δt′ = x₀ sinh (at) − 0 = x₀ sinh (at)  
Δx′ = x₀ cosh (at) − 0 = x₀ cosh (at)  
Δy′ = y₀ − 0 = y₀  
Δz′ = z₀ − 0 = z₀

Step 3 Insert these into the invariant interval in finite Cartesian form  
Δs² = −(Δt′)² + (Δx′)² + (Δy′)² + (Δz′)²  
        = −[x₀ sinh (at)]² + [x₀ cosh (at)]² + y₀² + z₀²

Step 4 Use the hyperbolic‐trigonometric identity cosh²θ − sinh²θ = 1  
[x₀ cosh (at)]² − [x₀ sinh (at)]² = x₀²[cosh²(at) − sinh²(at)] = x₀²

Hence  
Δs² = x₀² + y₀² + z₀².        

Step 5 Interpretation  
The invariant interval is positive (spacelike), so the proper distance D between any point on Γ and the origin is  

D = √(Δs²) = √(x₀² + y₀² + z₀²),

independent of the parameter t along the curve.

\boxed{D=\sqrt{x_{0}^{2}+y_{0}^{2}+z_{0}^{2}}}

Confidence: 91%
